data.title: SVN: Strong 2025 results with higher profit and revenue; euro reporting starts in 2026
data.description:

data.title: RTO: Revenue up 3.8% to $6.9bn, profit and cash flow improved, termite provisions increased
data.description:
Revenue grew 3.8% to $6,908m with 2.6% organic growth, driven by improved North America and International performance. Adjusted Operating Profit rose 5.4% to $1,070m, Free Cash Flow conversion hit 98%, and net debt fell to $3,650m. Termite claim provisions increased, and 36 acquisitions were completed.

Original document: Rentokil Initial plc [RTO] Annual Financial Statement — Mar. 5 2026

data.title: NEEPCO to issue staggered redemption bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's North Eastern Electric Power Corporation (NEEPCO) plans to raise at least 7.5 billion rupees ($81.85 million), including a greenshoe option of 5 billion rupees, through the sale of staggered redemption bonds maturing in 10 years, three bankers said on Thursday.

The state-run firm invited coupon and commitment bids for the issue on Monday, they said. The bonds will have a call option after five years, they added.

NEEPCO did not immediately respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.

Here is the list of deals reported so far on March 5:

Issuer

NEEPCO Staggered Redemption Bonds

Tenure

10 years

Bidding date

March 9

Rating

($1 = 91.6275 Indian rupees)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 1:05 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.64% at 79,621, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.66% to 24,640, with gains in Reliance and a global stock rally supporting the domestic market.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.6% versus the U.S. dollar to 91.62, lifted by aggressive RBI action and a steadier global backdrop, while traders cautioned the rebound could fade as geopolitical tensions continue to cast a pall on risk assets.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 98.8475 rupees, with the yield down 3 bps at 6.6434%, as likely central bank intervention in the debt and FX markets shored up sentiment. IN/]

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 1.25 basis points at 5.55%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was up 2.25 bps to 6.1125%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 4.85%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 4.79%.

data.title: ITV says Sky talks continue as it beats 2025 profit expectations
data.description:
ITV , the British TV company that is in talks to sell its broadcast unit to Sky, beat market forecasts with a 1% decline in 2025 adjusted operating profit and said advertising in the first quarter was better than expected.

The company on Thursday said it remained in discussions after it reported profit of 534 million pounds ($712 million), ahead of a consensus of 511 million pounds, on total group revenue of 4.12 billion pounds, unchanged from 2024.

The "Coronation Street" broadcaster said advertising in the first quarter of 2026 would be down around 2%, better than the 4.8% drop analysts had predicted, with brand owners holding back budget for the expanded soccer World Cup in the summer.

Chief Executive Carolyn McCall said two-thirds of revenue now came from its ITV Studios business and its digital platform, reducing its reliance on its linear TV channels.

"We have an exceptional content line-up for 2026, and are the only commercial streamer and broadcaster in the UK with rights to both the significantly expanded Men's Football World Cup, and every England Men's rugby match," she said.

ITV said in November it was in talks to sell its Media and Entertainment unit to pay-TV group Sky, owned by U.S. company Comcast , for 1.6 billion pounds ($2.18 billion).

"We remain in discussions with Sky regarding a possible sale of the M&E business. There can be no certainty as to whether a transaction will take place and an update will be made in due course," ITV said in its statement.

The talks have slowed in recent weeks, three people with knowledge of the matter told Reuters last month, as the battle to buy Warner Bros. Discovery disrupted the industry.

($1 = 0.7504 pounds)

data.title: Eurozone Government Bond Yields Open Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields rise in opening trade, tracking U.S. Treasurys and reversing the previous day's moves. Despite overall fragile investor sentiment, some signs point to stabilization ahead, with focus on economic data. "There are tentative signs that the front-end washout may be nearing an end, perhaps allowing a small role for incoming data," Citi's Jamie Searle and Puja Sawant say in a note. In a bigger-picture context, the rates strategists doubt that the energy price spike due to the Middle East conflict can reverse monetary-policy cycles. The 10-year German Bund yield rises 4 basis points to 2.784%, according to Tradeweb. Ten-year Spanish and French bond yield rise by slightly more ahead of respective bond auctions. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: China's Android Smartphone Shipments Might Continue to Drop — Market Talk
data.description: China's Android smartphone shipments might continue to drop, according to Jefferies analysts in commentary. Jefferies' industry check suggests that China's smartphone end-user sales in the year to late-February fell about 6% from a year earlier, driven by a decline of around 10% for Android smartphones and growth of about 15% for iPhones. This suggests inventory pressure is still high for Android smartphones, while iPhone's healthy inventory situation is due to strong demand, they say. "Given weak sellthrough for the Android camp, we believe Android shipments will likely continue to show a year-on-year decline," they say. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Iron ore jumps as China announces slew of economic measures
data.description:
By Ruth Chai

Iron ore futures advanced on Thursday as top consumer China announced a slew of economic measures that fueled optimism over steel and iron ore demand.

At the annual parliament meeting, the world's second-largest economy planned to boost domestic consumption and spur the housing market, while curbing steel overproduction, which has suppressed prices so far.

The most-traded May iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange traded 1.27% higher at 759 yuan ($110.09) a metric ton.

The benchmark April iron ore (SZZFJ6) on the Singapore Exchange was 1.03% higher at $99.8 a ton as of 0713 GMT.

As part of its 15th five-year plan, China set an economic growth target of 4.5%-5% for 2026, a slight downgrade from the 5% pace achieved last year but within analysts' expectations, leaving Beijing room to tackle weak domestic consumption and curb industrial overcapacity.

It also vowed to cut the production capacity of commodities, establish a carbon-emission control mechanism for industry and expand coverage of the national carbon-emission trading market over 2026-2030.

Additionally, the world's top steel exporter said that it would accelerate efforts to remove outdated production capacity and strengthen strategic resource reserves.

China will also strive to stabilise the real estate market, take steps to improve housing supply and better utilise existing housing stock, including by purchasing unsold homes for use as government-subsidised housing.

The country's protracted housing market used to be its biggest consumer of steel, but has since ceded its position to manufacturing. Stimulus targeting the property market would boost sentiment for steel and iron ore demand.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE drifted lower, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 0.36% and 0.06%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were mixed. Rebar firmed 0.1% and stainless steel rose 0.42%. Meanwhile, hot-rolled coil shed 0.19% and wire rod (SWRcv1) retreated 0.39%.

($1 = 6.8944 yuan)

data.title: ITV Looks to Cut Costs After Pretax Profit Fall
data.description:
(07:03 GMT) *ITV 2025 Adj. Pretax Pft GBP448M
(07:03 GMT) *ITV 2025 External Rev GBP3.51B
(07:03 GMT) *Analysts Saw ITV 2025 Rev at GBP3.52B
(07:03 GMT) *ITV 2025 Pretax Profit GBP338M
(07:04 GMT) *Analysts Saw ITV 2025 Pretax Profit at GBP361.8M
(07:04 GMT) *ITV External 2025 Rev Rose 3% on Year
(07:04 GMT) *ITV 2025 Adj. EBITDA GBP531M
(07:04 GMT) *ITV Proposes 2025 Dividend of 5,0 pence a Share
(07:05 GMT) *ITV Sees Further Revenue Growth in 2026
(07:06 GMT) *ITV Sees 2026 Adj. Ebita Margin At Lower end of 13%-15% Range on Revenue Mix
(07:07 GMT) *Correct: ITV 2025 External Rev GBP3.51B
(07:07 GMT) *ITV 2025 Rev 4.12B
(07:07 GMT) *Correct: ITV External 2025 Rev Rose 3% on Year
(07:08 GMT) *ITV 2025 Rev Flat on Year
(07:12 GMT) *ITV Sees ITV Studios Revenue Growing Ahead of Market in 2026
(07:33 GMT) ITV Looks to Cut Costs After Pretax Profit Fall

By Joshua Kirby

ITV PLC said pretax profit fell on year as the group moves to adjust its business to a changing media landscape.

Adjusted pretax profit came to 448 million pounds ($599.2 million) in 2025, down 5% from a year earlier, the U.K. media company said in its earnings update Thursday. Group revenue was flat on year at 4.12 billion pounds.

Still, revenue in digital grew 10%, helping offset a fall in advertising sales, ITV said.

For this year, ITV said it expects revenue in its TV business ITV Studios to outperform the market. The M&E streaming business should also book growth in its top line, the company said. The company aims to cut costs by 20 million pounds this year.

"We have created two resilient and attractive businesses, and ITV today is a demonstrably leaner, more agile and increasingly digital business, well adapted to deliver future growth," Chief Executive Carolyn McCall said.

The group will proposes an ordinary dividend for the year of 5 pence a share, up from 3.3 pence a year earlier.

The results come as ITV mulls offloading its broadcasting arm to rival Sky. Comcast's Sky late last year said it was in talks to buy the business for 1.6 billion pounds.

"We remain in discussions with Sky regarding a possible sale of the M&E business," the company said, adding that there could be no certainty that a transaction will take place.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: UK's Harbour Energy raises 2026 output view on new asset additions
data.description:
Britain's Harbour Energy raised its 2026 output outlook on Thursday, citing a strong start to the year, helped by early contributions from its newly acquired LLOG assets.

Harbour has been leaning on Wintershall Dea assets in Norway, Argentina and Mexico, while cutting investment and jobs in the UK over what it calls an "uncompetitive tax regime" - a concern heightened by Middle East turmoil, which has made the timing of ending the windfall tax less certain.

The company, however, posted a deeper after-tax loss of $182 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, widening from year-ago loss of $93 million, as its tax bill more than doubled to $2.98 billion from $1.31 billion a year earlier.

Harbour now expects 2026 production of 475,000-500,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day (boepd), compared with a prior estimate of 435,000-455,000 boepd.

The earlier outlook had excluded the impact of its acquisitions of Waldorf in the UK and LLOG in the U.S., as well as the announced sale of its Indonesian assets.
